atoms.source_snippet:
Self::Legacy(receipt)
            | Self::Eip2930(receipt)
            | Self::Eip1559(receipt)
            | Self::Eip7702(receipt) => receipt,
            Self::Legacy(receipt)
            | Self::Eip2930(receipt)
atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/optimism/primitives/src/receipt.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.snippet:
Left OP execution out of scope for this skeleton. Upstream Reth `v2.2.0` has
no `crates/optimism/*` workspace members and no manifest references to
`alloy-op-evm` or `op-revm` in this fresh tree.
destinations.evidence: The v2 port explicitly scopes out the old OP primitives crate that owned `OpReceipt`.
The active v2 port has Ethereum receipt support, but no OP-specific `OpReceipt` enum surface.

atoms.human_question: Confirm that OP receipt enum compatibility remains outside the current Reth v2.2/Summit merge scope. If OP support becomes in scope, this atom belongs to a dedicated OP primitives port.
atoms.notes: Reclassified from missing to intentionally absent because the current v2 port explicitly omits old `crates/optimism/*` workspace members.

summary: The old OP `OpReceipt` EIP-7702 handling is intentionally absent from the current v2 port because OP execution is out of scope.
notes: This remains a human scope confirmation for future OP work rather than an implementation gap in the active Summit/Ethereum port.
